strategy.north_star: Build the most reliable AI agent platform in the market, where reliability is the primary differentiator
strategy.thesis: In a crowded AI tooling market, reliability is undersupplied. 32% of organizations cite quality issues as their primary production barrier. By building reliability-first infrastructure with comprehensive evals, self-verification patterns, and production-grade observability, we can capture the enterprise segment that needs agents they can trust.
engineering_principles: Reliability over features: Ship less, but ship reliable
Eval-driven development: No feature ships without eval coverage
Self-verification by default: Agents check their own work before completion
Observable by design: Every agent action is traceable and auditable
Fail gracefully: Defined termination conditions and fallback behaviors
Cost-aware: Model routing and caching are first-class concerns
anti_goals: We will NOT chase feature parity with every competitor
We will NOT sacrifice reliability for speed-to-market on new capabilities
We will NOT build monolithic agents; prefer composable, testable units
We will NOT rely solely on prompt engineering; invest in structured evaluation
We will NOT optimize for demo-ware; optimize for production behavior

bets.name: Reliability-First Agent Framework
bets.rationale: 89% of orgs have observability but 32% still cite quality issues. The gap is in the framework itself, not just monitoring. Build agents that self-verify, produce legible artifacts, and have defined termination conditions. [ev-002, ev-005, ev-011]
success_criteria: Agent task completion rate > 95% on production workloads
Self-correction catches > 80% of errors before user impact
Zero runaway agent incidents (infinite loops, resource exhaustion)

bets.name: Comprehensive Evaluation Platform
bets.rationale: Amazon's 4-pillar framework (quality, performance, responsibility, cost) is the emerging standard. Build evaluation into CI/CD, not as an afterthought. [ev-004, ev-007, ev-008]
success_criteria: 100% of agent behaviors have eval coverage
Automated regression detection in CI pipeline
Quality score visible on every deployment

bets.name: Production Observability Stack
bets.rationale: Agent-specific observability captures behaviors traditional monitoring misses: reasoning chains, tool selection accuracy, output quality metrics. [ev-003, ev-009]
success_criteria: Full trace visibility for every agent execution

bets.name: Cost/Latency Optimization Layer
bets.rationale: 30-50% cost reduction achievable via model routing, semantic caching (96.9% latency reduction on hits), and prompt optimization. [ev-012, ev-013, ev-014]
success_criteria: 40% reduction in LLM spend vs. naive implementation
P95 latency < 2 seconds for standard operations
Cache hit rate > 60% for repetitive workloads

architecture.diagram_mermaid:
graph TB
    subgraph "Client Layer"
        SDK["Agent SDK"]
        CLI["CLI Tools"]
        API["REST API"]
    end

    subgraph "Gateway Layer"
        GW["LLM Gateway"]
        CACHE["Semantic Cache"]
        ROUTER["Model Router"]
    end

    subgraph "Agent Runtime"
        HARNESS["Agent Harness"]
        SANDBOX["Sandbox Container"]
        VERIFY["Self-Verification"]
        TOOLS["Tool Registry"]
    end

    subgraph "Evaluation Platform"
        EVAL["Eval Engine"]
        GOLDEN["Golden Datasets"]
        REGRESS["Regression Tests"]
        QUALITY["Quality Metrics"]
    end

    subgraph "Observability"
        TRACE["Trace Collector"]
        METRICS["Metrics Store"]
        ALERTS["Alert Manager"]
        DASH["Dashboards"]
    end

    subgraph "LLM Providers"
        CLAUDE["Claude API"]
        GPT["OpenAI API"]
        LOCAL["Local Models"]
    end

    SDK --> GW
    CLI --> GW
    API --> GW
    GW --> CACHE
    GW --> ROUTER
    ROUTER --> HARNESS
    HARNESS --> SANDBOX
    HARNESS --> VERIFY
    HARNESS --> TOOLS
    VERIFY --> EVAL
    EVAL --> GOLDEN
    EVAL --> REGRESS
    EVAL --> QUALITY
    HARNESS --> TRACE
    TRACE --> METRICS
    METRICS --> ALERTS
    METRICS --> DASH
    CACHE --> CLAUDE
    CACHE --> GPT
    CACHE --> LOCAL
    ROUTER --> CLAUDE
    ROUTER --> GPT
    ROUTER --> LOCAL

components.name: LLM Gateway
components.description: Unified entry point handling routing, caching, failover, rate limiting

components.name: Agent Harness
components.description: Runtime that manages agent lifecycle, tool execution, termination conditions
components.tech: Python/TypeScript based on Claude Agent SDK patterns

components.name: Self-Verification Module
components.description: Rules-based and LLM-based output verification before completion
components.tech: Integrated into harness, configurable per agent

components.name: Eval Engine
components.description: Automated evaluation against golden datasets, quality metrics, regression tests
components.tech: DeepEval or custom, integrated with CI/CD

components.name: Trace Collector
components.description: Captures full execution traces including reasoning chains, tool calls, costs

data_flows.data: Route to appropriate model based on complexity

q1.description: Core runtime with termination conditions, basic self-verification
Golden dataset infrastructure, basic quality metrics, CI integration
Trace collection, basic dashboards, alert framework

q2.description: LLM-based verification, configurable rule sets, feedback loops
Full regression suite, automated quality gates, developer dashboard
Production semantic cache with similarity tuning
Python and TypeScript SDKs with full harness integration

q3.description: Coordination patterns, shared state, handoff protocols
Reasoning chain visualization, cost attribution, drift detection

q4.description: Performance tuning, cost optimization, operational maturity
Behavioral testing, adversarial robustness, safety suite
Security audit, penetration testing, SOC2 prep

assumptions.assumption: Claude and GPT APIs remain stable and pricing competitive
assumptions.validation_method: Monitor provider announcements, maintain multi-provider capability

assumptions.assumption: 8 engineers sufficient for MVP in 3 months
assumptions.validation_method: Q1 velocity tracking, adjust scope if needed

assumptions.assumption: Semantic caching achieves claimed 60%+ hit rates on our workloads
assumptions.validation_method: Pilot with production traffic subset in Q1

assumptions.assumption: Enterprise customers will pay premium for reliability
No major regulatory changes affecting AI agent deployment in next 12 months
assumptions.validation_method: Monitor EU AI Act implementation, US executive orders
